scope.summary: Implement FabricMaterialHint derivation from GarmentSpec.natural_description and inject it into the Tailor ContextBundle so models receive a pre-resolved archetype hint without reasoning about raw compliance values
acceptance_criteria: A FabricMaterialHint struct MUST be defined carrying: suggested_archetype: Option<FabricPreset>, confidence: f32 [0,1], reasoning: String (max 120 chars for model legibility), and per-field nudges: Vec<(String, f32)> for specific FabricProperties adjustments

The hint derivation MUST be implemented as a deterministic keyword-matching pass over GarmentSpec.natural_description (not an LLM call) in handshake_core::tailor::material::derive_fabric_hint(), so it runs without model inference cost at every ContextBundle assembly

The keyword matcher MUST recognize at minimum: leather/suede->leather, silk/satin/charmeuse->silk, denim/jeans->denim, jersey/knit/stretch->jersey/spandex, chiffon/voile->chiffon, canvas/duck->canvas, wool/tweed->wool
ContextBundle for task='author_garment' MUST include the FabricMaterialHint in its allowed_context field alongside the workspace preset list and current panel FabricProperties

When natural_description is None or the keyword matcher finds no match, FabricMaterialHint.suggested_archetype MUST be None and confidence MUST be 0.0; the model MUST NOT receive a spurious archetype suggestion

The ModelManual entry for the author_garment context MUST document that FabricMaterialHint is present in allowed_context and explain that it is a keyword-derived suggestion, not a validated solver output

An integration test MUST assert that 'black leather corset with boning' -> FabricMaterialHint(suggested_archetype=Some(Leather), confidence >= 0.8) and 'flowing chiffon evening gown' -> FabricMaterialHint(suggested_archetype=Some(Chiffon), confidence >= 0.8)
scope.risk_if_missed: Without FabricMaterialHint, models authoring garments with clear material descriptions must independently reason about which FabricPreset to use from the flat preset list. This increases token usage and introduces preset-selection errors (e.g., a model choosing 'cotton' for a 'silk blouse' because the hint is absent). The spec explicitly requires this hint in the ContextBundle (§<N>.<i>.7.3); MT-276 builds the general ContextBundle shell but does not implement this material-specific injection. The omission degrades model-first garment authoring quality for every garment with a material description.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
